disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Dull, heavy, constant right-upper-quadrant ache, present for a while and worse over the past 48 hours, not sharp or colicky
trigger_question_examples: Can you describe the pain and where it is?
How long has this been going on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's up under my right ribs — a dull heavy ache, like pressure and fullness rather than a sharp pain. I've felt it on and off for a while but it's stepped up over the last two days.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Longstanding central obesity, type 2 diabetes and high cholesterol (metabolic risk cluster)
trigger_question_examples: Do you have any ongoing health conditions?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've got type 2 diabetes and high cholesterol, and I've carried a lot of weight around my middle for years. I take metformin and a cholesterol tablet.

trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fevers, chills or shivering?
Have you felt hot and sweaty or shaky?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't had any fevers or chills that I've noticed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No yellowing of the eyes or skin on the patient's own inspection (screened negative, self-observed)
trigger_question_examples: Have you noticed any yellowing of your eyes or skin?
Has anyone said you look yellow?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I looked in the mirror and I don't think my eyes or skin have gone yellow.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No vomiting and no sudden sharp or gripping pain (screened negative)
trigger_question_examples: Has the pain ever come on suddenly and sharply, or been unbearable?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No vomiting, and it's never been a sudden sharp pain — it's always been that dull heavy kind of ache.

trigger_question_examples: How have your energy levels been?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been exhausted all the time and it's really limiting what I can do.

trigger_question_examples: How much alcohol do you drink, on average?
Have you ever drunk heavily in the past?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I barely drink at all — never have, and never heavily.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No unintentional weight loss, no loss of appetite, no change in bowel habit (screened negative)
trigger_question_examples: Have you lost any weight without trying?
Any change in your appetite or bowel habits?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I haven't lost weight without trying and my appetite and bowels are pretty much the same as usual.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: High health anxiety with a fear of serious liver disease driving the presentation
trigger_question_examples: What are you most worried this could be?
How are you feeling about all this?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Honestly I'm terrified my liver is failing or diseased — that's why I couldn't just wait for a normal appointment.

patient_initiated_exchanges.trigger_condition: At any point the AI Doctor has not yet addressed the patient's central fear
patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Please just tell me — is my liver failing? Is this something that could kill me?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Acknowledges the fear without either dismissing it ('it's nothing') or catastrophising; explains that this dull ache with no fever, no yellowing and no vomiting is reassuring but that a proper answer needs an in-person examination and blood tests plus an ultrasound, which will be arranged promptly; gives clear red-flag safety-netting.

patient_initiated_exchanges.trigger_condition: If the AI Doctor raises the metabolic conditions or the statin
patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Should I stop my cholesterol tablet if my liver is the problem?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Advises NOT to stop the statin on their own — statins are generally safe and often beneficial in fatty-liver conditions, and any change is a decision for the GP after the bloods; continuing metformin and the statin is correct.
